title: naughty_strings() FILEPATH breaks when package installed from a wheel
body: naughtystrings/__init__.py computes FILEPATH as `dirname(dirname(__file__))/blns.txt`, assuming blns.txt sits next to the package source. When installed via `pip install big-list-of-naughty-strings` from a built wheel, blns.txt isn't necessarily copied to that relative location, so `naughty_strings()` raises FileNotFoundError for end users.

title: go:generate directive depends on archived jteeuwen/go-bindata
body: naughtystrings.go's go:generate comment still shells out to `github.com/jteeuwen/go-bindata/go-bindata`, but that repo has been archived/unmaintained for years and doesn't reliably `go install` on modern Go toolchains. Could the generator be swapped for a maintained embed-based approach (e.g. Go's native `//go:embed`)?

title: txt_to_json.py opens output file in binary mode but writes with json.dump
body: scripts/txt_to_json.py opens `blns.json` with `open('../blns.json', 'wb')` and then calls `json.dump(content, f, ...)`. On Python 3 this raises `TypeError: a bytes-like object is required, not 'str'` since json.dump writes str, not bytes. The script currently can't be run as-is on Python 3.

title: No documentation on when/how to regenerate blns.base64.txt
body: scripts/texttobase64.sh converts blns.txt into a base64 variant, but there's no comment explaining when contributors are expected to re-run it (e.g. after every blns.txt edit?) or what consumes blns.base64.txt downstream. Could a short usage note be added to the script header?

title: Go tests don't assert Base64Encoded() and Unencoded() stay in sync
body: naughtystrings_test.go exercises Base64Encoded() and Unencoded() individually, but nothing asserts the two slices have the same length/ordering, which is an implicit invariant since they're supposed to represent the same underlying string list in two encodings. A regression that only updates one of blns.json/blns.base64.json wouldn't be caught.
